meaning: n.<法>屠宰场,(拳击,摔跤,斗牛等的)角斗场
examples: We knew it was an abattoir when we arrived.
The inspection is usually carried out by designated veterinarians or meat inspectors at the abattoir.
A slaughterhouse also called an "abattoir", is a facility where animals are killed and processed into meat products.
The former abattoir opened as a shopping centre two years ago during the recession.
The abattoir's owner said no horse with false paperwork had ever knowingly been slaughtered there.
